TextENGNew:
"A Randy-kun doll?!"
"Ahh—yeah, of course that's right up Rena's alley!"
changeReason: New adds 'doll' (JPN 人形) to 'Randy-kun doll' which old omitted.

TextENGNew:
"...I can understand her wanting to take home Satoko and Rika-chan."
"But a Randy-kun doll? I'll never understand what she sees in that four-eyed old geezer!"

"Why not just ask Rena herself?"
"Though she'd probably only say, 'Because he's just so kyuuute☆.'"
"Does Rena often go to that construction site to hunt for treasure?"
"...She said she goes to have a look every now and then."
"Still... a girl her age rummaging through a garbage heap..."
"...As long as Rena is happy, I think it's fine."

"What is that place, anyway?"
"Was it a dam construction site?"
changeReason: Old asks 'What were they doing over there? Dam construction?' New asks 'What is that place, anyway? Was it a dam construction site?' JPN asks あそこは何だよ (what is that place), which matches the new version.

TextENGNew: It had been nagging at me since the night before, like a fish bone caught in my throat.
changeReason: Old uses 'itch I couldn't scratch'; new uses 'fish bone caught in my throat.' JPN says 魚の小骨 (fish bone), making the new version's metaphor correct.

TextENGNew: "Hahaha! It got cancelled a while back though."
"...Everyone banded together and fought against it."
I was startled to hear a combative word like "fought" come from Rika-chan's lips.

"That's right!"
"It was absolutely outrageous!"
"They just decided, unilaterally, that Hinamizawa would be drowned under the reservoir!"
And the fact that we're all still here now means that resistance movement bore fruit.
changeReason: Old says 'protest succeeded'; new says 'resistance movement bore fruit.' JPN 抵抗運動が実った specifically mentions a resistance movement, not just a protest.

TextENGNew:
"Sorry, sorry!"
"Did I keep you waiting? ...Did I?"
"My sincerest apologies, Keiichi-san. I simply needed a moment to powder my nose!"

"Satoko."
"Hold it until you really need to go."
"Everything comes out in one go—it's easy and fast."
``Eek... E-Eek...! What kind of thing is that to say to a maiden in the bloom of her youth?!?!``
changeReason: Old says 'maiden of unparalleled beauty'; new says 'maiden in the bloom of her youth.' JPN 花も恥らう乙女 refers to youthful modesty, not beauty.

TextENGNew: Who was she calling a maiden? No maiden worth the name would ever react like that.
And so the usual banter ensued.

"Now then."
"In accordance with club regulations, I put a proposal before the assembled members!"
"I propose that we admit one Keiichi Maebara-kun as a new member of our activities—what say you!?"
"Rena has no objection!"

"Ohohoho!"
"I wonder if a common peasant like him is worthy of being my opponent!"
changeReason: Old says 'is up to the task'; new says 'is worthy of being my opponent.' JPN 私の相手を務められるかしら specifically means 'worthy of being my opponent.'

TextENGNew: "...Both Satoko and I approve."
All three voiced their approval in response to Mion's query.

"Unanimous! Congratulations, Keiichi Maebara-kun."
"I hereby grant you the honor of sitting the entrance examination for our esteemed club!"

"Hold on, explain from the beginning!"
"I never said I wanted to join!"
"What kind of club is this, anyway?"

"What our club does, you see—in order to cope with the complexities of modern society, we engage in various activities under an ever-changing set of conditions."
"...Sometimes favorable."
"...Or otherwise adversarial...!!"
"...I'm not very strong, so... please don't single me out. Let's all get along."

"Rena, you're too soft!"
"The weak are devoured—that's simply the way of the world!"
...That was sounding ominous.@k ...So what kind of club was it, exactly?!
"...In short, it's a club where everyone plays games together."
Rika-chan was the only one who gave my question a straight answer.
In other words, this "club" was built entirely around Mion's hobby of collecting games.
Each day they played through one of the many games from Mion's vast collection.
Depending on the day, there were prizes for the overall winner and penalty games for last place—all manner of variations, apparently.

"Fair warning, though. This is nothing like playing house!"
"You'd do well to treat every single win or loss like your life depends on it!"

"W-What's wrong with just having fun...!?"
"Don't make it sound so intense."

"Rule number one of the club!! Aim only for first place!"
"Half-hearted play just because 'it's only a game' will not be tolerated!!!"

"Rule number two of the club!"
"Towards that end, you are obligated to give your absolute best effort!"
...The way Satoko said it, it sounded more like: use whatever means necessary.
"...Of course I will give my all as well."
"I'm not very strong either... but I do my very best."
I understood what they were all getting at.
In a game where everyone is competing for the top, playing just to have fun was a kind of unfairness to the others.
"All right, then—I'll give this everything I've got!!"
And so the time had come at last for my admission trial... my baptism into the club.

"A word of warning, Kei-chan."
"...Rena can get pretty merciless, you know."
The initiation trial looked like it was going to be brutal.
Mion was rummaging through the student lockers at the back of the classroom.
...They kept games at school?@k ...Well, I suppose it's club equipment, so that's fine.

"A difficult game would put Kei-chan at too much of a disadvantage."
"Today let's play something everyone knows."
"Something standard—how about Old Maid with a deck of cards?!"
"All right! I accept the challenge!!!"

"You can't really get fired up without a penalty on the line!"
"This time, last place gets their face drawn on! What do you think?!"

"Wh-Whoa... It won't be permanent marker, right?"
"...Not permanent marker... right...?"
...Permanent marker... Was she saying the face-drawing would be done with permanent marker...?
"That's perfectly acceptable!!!"
Satoko cheered the idea with relish.
Not even the prospect of such a severe penalty gave her pause.@k She was clearly raring to go.
...Well, I wasn't about to lose either!
"Then let me just cut the deck... and deal everyone in!"
Old Maid—called Old Bachelor here—is essentially the same game.
The one difference: there's no joker card.@k Instead, one card is secretly removed from the deck at the start.
That means the card whose pair was removed will inevitably be the last one left at the end.
No one knows which card has become the odd one out.@k It's a thrillingly suspenseful game!
"I'll take one card out now."
Mion removed one card and placed it face-down in the center of the circle.
Everyone stared intently at that card.

"Well, it'll become obvious in the endgame."
"Not much you can do at the start."
But... the others were deadly serious.@k There was none of the casual ease I felt.
They were comparing their hands to the face-down card and sizing up everyone around them.
It was almost as if... they could see through to the face-down card...
...Surely not...

"...This deck is pretty beat-up, isn't it."
"...No way... Can everyone identify the face-down card from its scratches...?"

"Remember rule number two～"
"Keiichi-san should make every effort to win as well～!"
"Several cards have distinctive marks... Keiichi-kun should be able to memorize them pretty quickly."
She said it so breezily.

"S-Suits me fine!!"
"Don't think a little thing like that can hold me back!!!!"
...I fired back defiantly, but it was plain as day that I was at a severe disadvantage.
After all... how much you'd memorized about each card's wear and markings was everything here...!
This was no longer a simple game of Old Maid.@k ...In mahjong terms, they were playing with marked tiles!!
changeReason: Old uses a test/answer sheet metaphor; new correctly translates the JPN mahjong reference ガン牌 (marked tiles). Different cultural reference entirely.

TextENGNew: Bring it on!@k I'll use your own complacency against you and beat you at your own game...!
...That baseless confidence of mine... would be swiftly demolished...

"Kekeke!"
"Shall I recite your whole hand starting from the right, Kei-chan?"
"Three, Four, Nine, Jack, Queen."
"GwAAAAH!!"
"...By the way, the bachelor card is the Jack of Diamonds."
"GuAAARgh!!"

"No matter how you rearrange them, they're perfectly transparent to me!"
"I'm out!!"
"UuuRRRgh!!"
I'd braced myself... but this was just... overwhelming...!!!

"D... Demons... You're all demons...!"
"Rena... you're not... a demon... are you...?"

"S-Sorry, Keiichi-kun."
"...That's the Three of Hearts, isn't it? ...I'm out!"
"UwAAAAaaAaaAAAAaah!!"
Not an ounce of mercy...!!@k Even Rena... even Rika-chan...!!
These club activities were not to be trifled with.@k Any alumnus of this club could surely survive the harshest of conditions...
If the whole class were dropped onto an uninhabited island with self-detonating collars strapped to their necks, these people would probably thrive.
I could already picture their glee as they hunted down one classmate after another...

"It's a point deduction system."
"Your finishing order directly becomes your minus points. Whoever has the fewest points at the end wins!"
"...Since Keiichi came in last, that's five points against him."
A deduction of 5 was already being written down on my scoreboard...!
"...I-I think it's unfair to Keiichi-kun not to use a fresh deck..."
"It's fine, it's fine. Kei-chan is a man after all.
He can overcome a little adversity like this, right?!"
"Scurrying home in tears to cry into his pillow is more befitting a common peasant like him, wouldn't you say??"
As I trembled with helpless frustration, a small hand came to rest gently on my head.@k ...It was Rika-chan's.
"...Keep at it, yeah～."
Rika-chan's encouragement helped me finally regain my composure.
Stay calm, especially when your back is against the wall.@k ...That was the "strength" I could call on in moments of crisis.
Think clearly, Keiichi Maebara.
...Take your time and study the cards carefully.
Just as Rena had said, several cards had unmistakably distinctive markings—memorizing them looked easy enough.
Think through what options you have right now. Try every possible angle to improve your chances of winning.
"Wow... You're doing great, Keiichi-kun. Keep it up!"
I covered the distinctively marked cards with my hands or overlapped them with other cards so their features couldn't be seen.

"...The one with the torn corner should be the Five."
"...I'm out."
I couldn't afford to miss a single stray comment from the other players—every scrap of information was a weapon.
Then it was my turn.
Satoko ostentatiously fanned her cards out and thrust them toward me.

"What I want is a Seven."
"...Would it be this one?"

"...I wonder about that...??"
"You'll just have to pick and see...?"
A flicker of unease crossed Satoko's expression.@k ...As if she really didn't want that card taken... That kind of look...!
I wasn't going to let that split-second slip pass me by!!

"I can see it!!"
"It's THIS oooooone!!!!"
Got it!
A cheer of "Ohhh!" burst out!

"W-Wh-What?!"
"The Seven should be the hardest one to identify of all～?!?!"
Not from memorizing the markings—the subtle shifts in their expressions as I chose were more than enough of a clue!

"You can't hide it from me, Kei-chan!"
"...This mark means it's the Two of Diamonds."
She was stunned by her own misread.@k And of course so was everyone else...!
"...Whoa... It's rare for Mii-chan to get a card wrong..."

"N-No..."
"Kei-chan... Could it be that you...!!"
They identified the cards by their "marks."@k And among those marks were "nail scratches."
So... I had added one of my own.

"Are you saying you disguised it... as the Two of Diamonds?!"
"W-What a brazen piece of trickery...!!"
"...Keiichi has landed a blow."
Clap... clap... clap...

"You did it, Keiichi-kun!"
"That was an incredible showing, wasn't it?! Wasn't it?!"
Having taken down the ringleader Mion, I was floating on air from my own brilliant display.
But in terms of total points, it was already a foregone conclusion—Mion first, me dead last.
I laughed with a carefully chosen tone of voice, one calculated to get under Mion's skin.

"...Well... last place is a given, but getting one over on Mion at the very end has me fully satisfied.☆"
"Bwahahahahahaa～...♪"
For Mion, already locked into total victory, that had to sting...!
"Even if Kei-chan wins this round, last place overall is still his... You hate that, don't you?"
"Of course I do!"

"Let's have a one-on-one duel."
"One shot!"
"If you win, we flip first and last—a massive reversal! ...What do you say?! You're in, right?!"
Hooked!
The only way to manufacture a comeback was by goading the winner into this!!

"One shot, you say?"
"Tell me the rules."
Mion discarded two cards from her hand and added the joker that had been sitting on the table, then shuffled the resulting hand behind her back.

"The card in my right hand and the card in my left!"
"If Kei-chan can guess which one is the joker, he wins!"
"When you turned around just now—you didn't slip the joker away, did you?!"

"If you lose, I'll show you the card in the other hand."
"That ought to settle it, right?"
The other three swallowed hard at this unexpected final gamble.

"...All right!!"
"You're on!!!!"
Mion grabbed one card in each hand and thrust them out at me!
First things first—I needed to study them carefully...!
The card on the right had no distinctive marks. I couldn't make heads or tails of it.
"...I wonder which one... Keiichi-kun... be careful!"
"Yeah, of course! ...This is my one chance... at a comeback!!"
Looking closely at the card on the left, I noticed some faint wear. It seemed familiar—as if I'd seen it somewhere before.
"Ah... that card is...!!"
I didn't let even that stray murmur from Satoko slip past me.
Mion clicked her tongue.
The mark on the left card didn't match any of the handful I'd managed to memorize.
But judging from Satoko's reaction, whatever the left card was, it had already appeared during the game.
If it had already appeared during play, it was definitively not the joker!

"...Hmm."
"Kei-chan suspects the one on the right?"
"So will you go with the right?"
"The right?"
So then—what to do...?
...If I could deduce that the left card was absolutely not the joker, then I ought to be able to declare with certainty that the right card was the joker.
Even without Mion's provocation—...the right was the most suspicious.

But wasn't it too rash to base my conclusion on Satoko's reaction alone?!
More carefully... slowly... Ah!

...I remember now...!!
That mark... the left card... there's no mistake!
The Seven of Clubs!!!

"Mmm..."
"I'm pretty sure that's the Seven of Clubs..."

I've got it!
The left card is the Seven of Clubs!
Which means the remaining right card is... the Joker!!!!
And then—just as my hand was about to touch the right card, I stopped dead.

"...Heh heh heh... heh heh heh..."
"Exactly what I'd expect from you, Mion."
The murmurs rippled through the room at my sudden pause—every last person had been certain the right card was the joker.

"?? Huh?"
"What on earth could Keiichi-san be saying??"
"But the left one is..."

"Oh...?"
"What makes you so certain the right one isn't the joker, Kei-chan?"
An unexpected remark from Mion.@k Everyone except me was thrown into confusion.
changeReason: Old says 'Everyone else was baffled'; new says 'Everyone except me was thrown into confusion.' JPN 俺を除く全員 explicitly says 'everyone except me' which old omitted.

TextENGNew:
"I have no idea what the right card is."
"But what I do know is that the left card is the Seven of Clubs."

"Then!"
"Doesn't that leave the right one as the joker?!"
"One of the two has to be the joker—that was the deal, wasn't it?!"

"Yeah."
"...One of the two cards is the joker—that much is certainly true."
"...Keiichi's intuition is sharp."

"Huh?"
"Rika-chan, what do you mean by...?"

"The thing is—"
"...the Seven of Clubs."
"...That's the card I discarded a moment ago when it matched Satoko's Seven of Spades...!"
Everyone's eyes snapped to the discard pile in unison!
In all that jumbled mess, there was no way to verify the truth...!

"In other words—"
"...Mion picked up one card from the discard pile... and layered it on top of the left card... which means...!!!"

"...I see, I get it!"
"The left card... is being disguised as the Seven of Clubs, isn't it?!"
For the first time since the game began, a shadow crossed Mion's face—and I caught it.
And then... with a dramatic spin and flourish, I made my declaration!!
"The joker is... the LEFT haaaaaaaand!!!!!"

A scorching-hot moment...!
That single second felt like hours to everyone in the room.
The silence broke—and the first one to speak was... Mion.

"...As founding club president, I've witnessed all manner of performances."
"...Kei-chan."
"...You are... the best."
"...The best of the best of the best!"
Probably the highest compliment Mion was capable of giving.@k She admitted defeat... and let both cards fall from her hands.
...My... comeback victory!!!!!!
Rika-chan placed her hand softly on my head, celebrating my reversal victory.

"Huh...?"
"...Rika-chan... is...?"
Rena and Satoko stared at us with blank, wide-eyed expressions... as though they'd seen a ghost.
"What is it, Rena? It's not like I cheated or anything, right?? Fair and square!"
"...Rika only pats someone on the head... when she's consoling them."
Huh...? What did that mean...?
At that moment, Rena let out a short shriek.

"K-Keiichi-kun...!"
"...How could this be...!?!?"

"You know, Kei-chan."
"I thought you'd see through it that far."
"...Heheheheh!"
Everyone froze.@k ...Or was it just me...

"It was a gamble."
"If you'd jumped to conclusions like a hothead, I would have lost."
...It was the answer everyone had been convinced of...
Mion picked up the card from the losing hand.
"Kei-chan—you have every right to be proud of this loss."
She turned it over...
...It was... the card that declared the victor of this duel.
Hidden beneath the Seven of Clubs was... none other than the Two of Diamonds!

I-I was had at my own game...!!
If I'd just picked the right one without overanalyzing it...!

"...In the name of club president Mion Sonozaki."
"...Keiichi Maebara."
"...I hereby grant your admission... to our club...!!!"
The moment I crumpled to my knees in a daze, everyone gave the game a thunderous round of applause.
changeReason: Old says 'for the excellent effort I gave despite my defeat'; new says 'gave the game a thunderous round of applause.' JPN この好ゲーム (this good game) focuses on the game itself, not Keiichi's effort.

TextENGNew:
"That settles it!!"
"Today's champion is me! Mion Sonozaki!! And our glorious last place finisher is... Keiichi Maebara～!!!"
Everyone cheered and clapped with delight.
I felt the sting of defeat, but it was such a clean, precise cut that it almost felt bracing...
"When Mii-chan did that trick behind her back, I thought, 'She's doing it again~!' My heart was pounding!"
"When Keiichi-san stopped right before touching the right card, I thought, 'He took the bait!～'"
"...He took the bait quite splendidly."
...Huh?
Wait a moment.
You all knew from the very beginning and still got so genuinely worked up like that...??
"...We all got excited to make it more fun for everyone."
"You're all DEMONS!! You're monsters～!!!"

"Now then, time for Kei-chan's penalty game...!"
"It is your first day in the club, after all."
"I'll go easy on you."
"...It would be a problem if you stopped coming to school over this!"
The other three pinned my arms and shoulders firmly in place.
Mion advanced on me, licking her lips...
Her right hand was reaching into her pocket to pull something out.
That's... a marker!! And not just any marker—an oversized permanent one!!
"Now then... here we gooo～!"
Mion popped the cap off the marker and inched its tip slowly toward my immobilized face...!
"N-No, no, stooooop...o...o...!!!"
My death cry echoed through the room...
